title: Kamelinia kopetdaghensis
intro: Kamelinia kopetdaghensis is a type of plant, specifically a species of shrub, native to the Kopet Dag mountain range in Turkmenistan. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Kamelinia kopetdaghensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to promote digestive health and reduce inflammation.

This classification is based on its characteristics and morphological features. The genus Kamelinia is often placed in the subfamily Apioideae, a group of plants that are typically found in temperate and subtropical regions.
